original_hypothesis.title: Unbounded Referral Fee Setting Can DoS All Swaps via Arithmetic Underflow
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: PairFactory.setReferralFee and setCustomReferralFee have no upper bound validation. If MAX_REFERRAL_FEE or a custom referral fee is set > 10000, then in Pair._update0/_update1, `_referralFee = amount * _maxRef / 10000` exceeds `amount`, causing `amount -= _referralFee` to underflow and revert (Solidity 0.8.13). This permanently DoSes all swaps on affected pairs.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: setReferralFee has zero validation (unlike setFee which checks <= MAX_FEE). A value of 10001 makes _referralFee > amount, causing underflow revert in _update0/_update1 which is called from every swap.

proof_of_signal.pattern: setReferralFee(uint256 _refFee) has no require(_refFee <= 10000) or similar bound; compare to setFee which has require(_fee <= MAX_FEE)

preconditions: feeManager calls setReferralFee with value > 10000 (accidentally or maliciously)
dibs address is set (non-zero) so referral fee logic is active
Any swap occurs on any pair

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: setReferralFee has no bounds check while setFee does; asymmetric validation
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: All swaps DoS'd from the moment the value is set

operational_questions: What is the current MAX_REFERRAL_FEE value on-chain?
Are there any custom referral fees set for specific pairs?
Could a feeManager accidentally set an invalid value?
validation_requirements: Set MAX_REFERRAL_FEE to 10001 — verify all swaps revert
Verify no upper bound check exists in either setReferralFee or setCustomReferralFee

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: feeManager calls setReferralFee(10001) — either accidentally or via compromised key
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All swaps on all pairs with dibs != address(0) are permanently DoSed

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: All swap operations DoSed on all pairs
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: feeManager must call setReferralFee with valid value; if feeManager key is lost, protocol is permanently DoSed
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: All swap operations across the protocol

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Sudden swap revert rate increase to 100%

validation_result.mechanism: Missing upper bound on referral fee causes arithmetic underflow DoS
validation_result.impact_summary: If feeManager sets MAX_REFERRAL_FEE or a custom referral fee > 10000, all swaps on affected pairs permanently revert due to arithmetic underflow in _update0/_update1.
validation_result.detailed_analysis: The hypothesis correctly identifies a missing bounds check in PairFactory.setReferralFee and setCustomReferralFee. While setFee validates `_fee <= MAX_FEE` and setStakingFees validates `_newFee <= 3000`, setReferralFee has zero validation on the input value. If MAX_REFERRAL_FEE is set to a value > 10000, then in Pair._update0/_update1, `_referralFee = amount * _maxRef / 10000` produces a value greater than `amount`. The subsequent `amount -= _referralFee` underflows in Solidity 0.8.13 (which has built-in overflow/underflow checks), causing a revert. Since _update0/_update1 are called from every swap, this effectively DoSes all trading on all pairs where dibs != address(0). The same issue applies to setCustomReferralFee for pair-specific overrides.
validation_result.code_evidence_assessment: Direct code evidence confirms the defect. PairFactory.setReferralFee at lines 57-59 has no bounds check. PairFactory.setCustomReferralFee at lines 72-75 also has no bounds check on _refFee. In Pair._update0, the subtraction `amount -= _referralFee` at line ~170 will underflow when _referralFee > amount, which occurs when referral fee > 10000. Compare with setFee which has `require(_fee <= MAX_FEE)` and setStakingFees which has `require(_newFee <= 3000)`.

vulnerable_code_snippets.vulnerability_explanation: No upper bound validation on _refFee. Any value including > 10000 is accepted, unlike setFee which checks _fee <= MAX_FEE.
No upper bound validation on _refFee for custom per-pair referral fees.
When _maxRef > 10000, _referralFee = amount * _maxRef / 10000 > amount, causing arithmetic underflow on `amount -= _referralFee` in Solidity 0.8.13.
Same underflow issue as _update0 for token1 fees.

calling_sequence: 1. User calls Pair.swap(amount0Out, amount1Out, to, data)
2. swap calculates amount0In from balance delta
4. _update0 calls PairFactory.getReferralFee(address(this)) which returns MAX_REFERRAL_FEE or customReferralFees
execution_path_analysis.vulnerable_code_path: Pair.swap → _update0/_update1 → PairFactory.getReferralFee → underflow on subtraction. The swap function at Pair.sol calls `_update0(amount0In * PairFactory(factory).getFee(address(this), stable) / 10000)`. Inside _update0, `uint256 _maxRef = PairFactory(factory).getReferralFee(address(this))` fetches the unbounded value. Then `uint256 _referralFee = (amount * _maxRef / 10000)` computes a value > amount when _maxRef > 10000. The line `amount -= _referralFee` triggers Solidity 0.8.13 checked arithmetic underflow revert.

config_validation.deployment_evidence: PairFactory.initialize sets MAX_REFERRAL_FEE = 0. No deployment scripts set MAX_REFERRAL_FEE for PairFactory. The setReferralFee in deployment scripts (setReferralFeeInGaugeFactory.js) targets gaugeFactoryCL, not PairFactory. The code defect exists regardless of initial config since there is no bounds check preventing future misconfiguration.

validation_result.attack_scenario_validation: The attack scenario is valid: (1) feeManager calls setReferralFee(10001) — either accidentally or via compromised key. (2) In Pair._update0: _referralFee = amount * 10001 / 10000 which is > amount for any non-zero amount. (3) amount -= _referralFee causes arithmetic underflow revert. (4) All swaps on all pairs with dibs != address(0) are DoSed. Recovery requires feeManager to call setReferralFee with a valid value ≤ 10000. The same attack works per-pair via setCustomReferralFee.
validation_result.impact_assessment: If exploited, this causes a complete trading halt on all pairs (when MAX_REFERRAL_FEE > 10000 and dibs is set) or specific pairs (when customReferralFees[pair] > 10000). The protocol cannot process any swaps until the feeManager corrects the value. If the feeManager key is lost or compromised without recovery, the DoS is permanent. This is a code defect (missing input validation) even though exploitation requires privileged access.
recommended_mitigations: Add bounds check to setReferralFee: require(_refFee <= 10000, 'HFE');
Add bounds check to setCustomReferralFee: require(_refFee <= 10000, 'HFE');
Consider a more conservative maximum like require(_refFee <= 1200) to cap referral fees at 12%
Add a timelock to fee parameter changes to allow users to react to potentially malicious changes
validation_result.replication_plan: 1. Deploy PairFactory with PairGenerator. 2. Create a pair with two test tokens. 3. Add liquidity to the pair. 4. Set dibs to a non-zero address via setDibs. 5. Call setReferralFee(10001) as feeManager. 6. Attempt a swap on the pair — expect revert with arithmetic underflow. 7. Verify recovery by calling setReferralFee(0) and confirming swaps work again.
uncertainties: The exact value of dibs on-chain is unknown — if dibs is address(0), referral fee logic is skipped and swaps still work
On-chain current MAX_REFERRAL_FEE value is unknown but initialized to 0
additional_testing_needed: Verify current on-chain MAX_REFERRAL_FEE value and dibs address for all deployed PairFactory instances
Check if any customReferralFees have been set for specific pairs
Test edge case where referral fee is exactly 10000 (100% of fees go to referral, 0 to LPs — legal but economically harmful)
